rationale: Temporal dissociation / dream-like detachment ("living in a dream where I could remember what I had just done 3 seconds ago… couldn't live in the present"). No modality, no object, no concrete perceptual content — phenomenologically real but arguably not a hallucinatory scene in the external-object sense of the other _AB scenes in this trip. Would fit a dissociative / altered-time-perception category the taxonomy lacks. Neither _AB scene here addresses time perception, so the rater criterion is silent on this case.

Mixed summary passage ("laws of physics changed, faces had changed, every room I couldn't see had to be a room in my house"). The face-alteration clause matches B's criterion (B individuated S06, an identity-morph scene), but the passage is framed as an ongoing summary of trip-state rather than a discrete perceptual moment, mixed with delusional content (laws of physics, room mapping). Guidelines do not cleanly rule on whether generalised perceptual-state summaries count as a scene or should be trip-level — an edge case needing a clarifying rule.

Concrete brief visual alteration ("strange colors for a moment"). The pair's criterion elsewhere (Brugmansia_01 S04 "blurriness and colors changing") includes ambient colour alterations, even brief ones. A should have individuated by own criterion.

Dislocation feeling ("somehow in the wrong place"). Metacognitive/dissociative state about place, not a perceptual hallucinatory scene. Phenomenologically real but outside the hallucinatory-scene category — would need a dissociative-experience category.

Auditory/social hallucination: conversing with a friend who wasn't really there. Matches the pair's criterion across every other Brugmansia trip (conversations with non-existent people are individuated consistently as _AB).
Concrete visual+social hallucination: a fake inmate in the police car becomes a friend, then vanishes. Classic person-hallucination pattern consistently individuated elsewhere.
Multiple visitors to jail cell "that weren't there". Concrete person hallucinations matching pair's criterion.
Post-trip reading impairment (letters visible but not forming words). Cognitive / residual-effects state, not a hallucinatory scene. Not a perceptual hallucination under any reasonable reading.

Hands appear red in shower — narrative adds "actually, they're still red", suggesting possibly real physiological flush rather than hallucinated colour. Edge case: Guidelines do not rule cleanly on how to handle colour perceptions that may persist after the trip as real phenomena.

Hallucinated text on shower wall ("you are not..."), fades when approached. Concrete visual hallucination with classic fade-on-approach dynamics. Strongly matches B's criterion across the rich AB set in this trip (hallucinated text, fading objects).

Vague summary: "watched all sorts of things fly by". No specific object, modality, or concrete content. Guidelines do not rule on whether non-specific generalised hallucination summaries constitute a discrete scene.

Text on screen shifting colours (grey/red/orange/yellow/blue/violet). Concrete visual alteration matching AB criterion (S02 "green/yellow waves of light on ceiling", S15 "walls pink-red"). B should have individuated.
"Loud crash near my bed" (no apparent source). Concrete auditory hallucination. Pair's criterion consistently includes auditory hallucinations (music, voices) elsewhere.
Ambient perceptual amplification without a discrete hallucinatory object — the Guidelines do not rule cleanly on whether brightness/colour/shimmer changes alone constitute a scene.

Tactile alteration: "everything touched feels strangely new". General tactile heightening without discrete object. Only _AB scene in this trip is a perceptual dysfunction (depth perception), so criterion for tactile is not established. Guidelines unclear on whether diffuse tactile "feels strange" sensations constitute a scene.

Water bottle disappears as it gets to his mouth. Classic disappearing-object hallucination. Pair's criterion includes many disappearing-object scenes elsewhere (Brugmansia_09_S03 CD player, Brugmansia_12_S03 pizza).
Friends S and T appear, have a full conversation, then vanish along with cigarettes. Concrete people+conversation hallucination, matches pair's criterion overwhelmingly.
Play structure parts anthropomorphised (flipping off / smiling / shadow becomes cloud dragon). Multiple concrete visual anthropomorphisations. Clear hallucinatory scene by any reasonable criterion.
Post-trip reading impairment ("cannot read the rest of the night"). Cognitive residual-effects state, not a hallucinatory scene. Same pattern as Brugmansia_02_S06.
"Everything's kind of turning to jello visually" — concrete whole-environment visual distortion. Matches AB S01 (flashes of light / sparkles) as ambient visual alteration criterion in this trip.

Converses with a companion "J" then realises J is 30 feet behind — had been talking with a non-existent companion. Near-identical to AB S04 "talking to imaginary people in the tent". B's omission is a clear compliance gap.
CD player disappears when looked at again. Classic disappearing-object hallucination. Directly matches AB S05 ("bread turns into marker and disappears") — same pattern.
Believed his parents had come home and had an imaginary greeting/interaction with them. Concrete person hallucination. Matches AB S03/S04 (imaginary people conversations, 5-6 friends hanging out).

Cognitive orientation error on waking ("thinking I was at my girlfriend's house"). Not a perceptual hallucination; a transient spatial-orientation delusion. Phenomenologically real but outside the hallucinatory-scene category.
Pen in hand perceived as the sandals he was looking for. Object-misidentification illusion. Matches AB criterion (many misperceptions: mirror-reflection, imaginary cigarettes, brake lights).
Friends in music store disappear when he looks for them. Concrete visual hallucination of people vanishing. Matches AB S03 (bathroom friends vanish) and S10 (girlfriend appears/disappears) directly.
Billiard ball perceived as an apple, almost bitten. Object-misidentification illusion, concrete visual. Matches AB criterion (imaginary cigarettes, guitar-playing hallucination, imaginary people).

Own mirror reflection perceived as a friend, has a conversation with "him" until hand-gesture mirroring reveals it's himself. Concrete visual+identity hallucination. Matches the only AB scene (2-hour conversation with imaginary friends) — same phenomenon.

Mistook canola cooking oil for a drink and drank it. Cognitive-judgement / perceptual-confusion error driven by impaired state, but not a discrete hallucinatory scene (no specific hallucinated content was perceived in the oil bottle). Pair's criterion in neighbouring trips is concrete perceptual hallucinations; this sits outside that category.

"The strange silent people would take on more horror-based figures" — summary statement describing evolution of AB S02/S03 silent-people scenes. Granularity edge-case: is this a new scene or a continuation of already-individuated scenes? Guidelines do not specify how to handle scene-evolution.

Mental patients on floor beside bed AT HOME (after hospital). Discrete continuation-at-new-location of S03 mental-patient hallucinations. A's individuation stopped at the earlier setting; B correctly individuated the home episode. A's omission is a gap.
Two specific female mental patients at home (pillow-headed and clock-headed). Concrete, specific visual hallucinations with distinctive morphology. Matches criterion clearly; A missed.

Tactile hallucination: feels someone's legs across his chest as if a person came in and slept in his bed. Concrete somatic-person hallucination. Matches AB criterion (people appearing in room / chair) in tactile modality.

"Couldn't see all to well because it was almost pitch black" — darkness / low-light impaired vision. Not a hallucinatory scene under any reasonable reading; the phenomenon (dark room) is real. A's individuation here is a miscode; B correctly did not individuate. Not a MISS by B — the content is outside the hallucinatory-scene category entirely.

"Returning to room still sees the person in the bed; then gone" — continuation of S01 chest-legs somatic hallucination, now visually. Concrete person hallucination. Matches AB S03/S05 (person in chair/on floor) criterion.
Roach on window blinds. Concrete insect hallucination. Directly matches AB S09 ("wasps flying in room; frog on blind-string eats a wasp") — same location, same modality, same category.

Morning light arriving in discrete brightness-waves rather than continuously. Temporal/perceptual alteration without discrete object. Guidelines unclear on whether experientially-discrete light changes constitute hallucinations vs subjective time-perception artefacts.

"Main narrator voice + other voices + entity voices + electronic music" — expands AB S01 (cosmic hum) and S05 (talks with people) with novel auditory layers. Granularity edge-case: is this a new auditory scene or elaboration of S01/S05? Guidelines do not specify how to split ongoing-auditory phenomena into discrete scenes.

"Erotic dance with entities; spotted by outsiders; giggling" — elaboration of AB S04 (entities in room) with specific interactive content. Granularity edge-case: is this a new scene or a continuation/inner episode of S04?

"Breaking edges visible again when focusing later in the trip" — explicit recurrence of the AB S02 kaleidoscopic-edges phenomenon. Guidelines do not rule on whether recurrences of a named phenomenon later in the trip are new scenes.

"Visualises mycelium branching in disturbed earth connecting all beings" — inner visualisation/metaphor accompanying an insight, not an external perceptual hallucination. Would fit an "insight content" or "visualised metaphor" category outside the hallucinatory-scene framework.

"Do you see how colourful is the sky?" — concrete ambient colour alteration. Near-identical to AB S10 ("Mexican environment perceived as extraordinarily colourful"). B should have individuated by own criterion on S10.

"Hands transparent, milky, blue veins, tiny particles moving" — concrete visual hallucination of own body. Matches rich AB criterion of concrete visual alterations; body-hallucinations are squarely within pair's criterion.

"Fell into a fantasy world like The Lord of The Rings" — closed-eye fantasy imagery. AB criterion is largely open-eye perceptual alteration. Guidelines unclear on whether closed-eye fantasy immersions constitute hallucinatory scenes.

"Taken back by a violent scream" — passage is ambiguous between real auditory event (girlfriend screamed) and auditory hallucination. Cannot determine from narrative alone; edge case requiring a rule on how to code ambiguous-source sounds.

Walking in wood: "brilliant green heart-shaped leaves, rain like ice needles, wind in dolby surround" — amplified multi-modal perceptual experience of environment. Matches AB S10 (colourful Mexican env.) and the perceptual-amplification pattern across the trip.

"California party with lots of people and music; amplified echo-sounds" — auditory hallucination creating displaced-place feeling. Concrete auditory content. Matches AB S09 (feeling of someone following) and the auditory-alteration pattern.
"Visions of my past, parties, clubs" — memory imagery / recollection with vividness. Not an external perceptual hallucination; would fit a memory-imagery category rather than hallucinatory-scene.

"Our scent is so strong… too strong, like animals" — olfactory hallucination/amplification with specific content. Pair's criterion is modality-agnostic across the trip (includes tactile feeling, visual, auditory).
"Hears voices outside as Chinese speakers under bamboo shelter" — concrete auditory hallucination with narrative content and embedded imagined setting. Clearly within criterion.

"Suddenly I entered 2001: A Space Odyssey" — immersive mental experience invoking film imagery. Edge-case between closed-eye immersive imagery (arguably AMBIGUITY-2a, like S05) and a cultural-reference metaphor.
Wood-board ceiling: "veins and nodes floating" — concrete visual surface-animation. Directly matches AB S02 ("tree with animated cortex") and S04 ("ground as moving worms").

"Everything turns silver; opera-set leans toward audience" — concrete visual alteration of whole environment. Matches AB S16 ("wall becomes liquid and transparent") — same category of environmental transformation.

Thought / memory / metaphor content individuated as a scene by one rater — the passage is phenomenologically real but arguably a different (non-perceptual) phenomenon the current taxonomy has no dedicated category for.

"Palm lines change colour, iridescent pulsing lines, hand as planet surface, dead hand" — concrete visual hallucination of own body with specific content. Matches body-alteration criterion; equal detail to AB scenes.

"Sees himself on a very high bridge pirouetting like a Cirque du Soleil acrobat" — concrete visual self-hallucination with specific content. Matches AB S03 (sees spirits and a pesky demon) as concrete visual content; an early-onset scene A might have discounted as too early.
Ambient perceptual amplification without a discrete hallucinatory object — the Guidelines do not rule cleanly on whether brightness/colour/shimmer changes alone constitute a scene.

"Pictures himself as a small child lost in space, flying into the nether" — inner imaginal metaphor / self-imagery. Not external perceptual content; would fit an "inner imagery" category outside hallucinatory-scene.

"Sun rising and setting repeatedly in background of picture; every component a breathing mass of cosmic sludge" — concrete visual hallucinated transformation of a viewed image. Directly matches AB S01 ("words on screen shimmer, mutate rhythmically") and S05 ("golden sun of geometric figures etched into screen") — same device.
"Sinister cosmic black hole pulling away more and more of my ego" — subjective ego-dissolution metaphor, no perceptual content. Classic trip-level phenomenon, not a hallucinatory scene.
Auditory hallucinations infiltrating thoughts ("ghost voices of acquaintances"). Concrete auditory hallucinations with specific source attribution. Matches criterion (multimodal AB content).
"Felt as though I had stumbled upon some cosmic isle free of earthly properties" — subjective feeling of being elsewhere, no concrete perceptual content. Classic trip-level atmospheric phenomenon.

"Speaking and hearing spoken words began to feel somewhat alien" — metacognitive/phenomenological feeling about language experience. No concrete perceptual hallucination. Would fit an altered-cognitive-phenomenology category.

"Couldn't see or make out colors or shapes; everything was a black and brown haze" after entering dark bathroom from bright sun. Normal dark-adaptation physiology, not a hallucination. Phenomenologically real but not a hallucinatory scene.
"Everything was kind of glassy" — ambient visual quality alteration. Matches AB S01/S04/S06 — all ambient surface-visual alterations in this trip.

"Period-rush: for a few seconds I felt like I was really there, in prehistoric earth in front of a T-rex" — brief immersive mental/temporal experience. Edge-case between immersive mental imagery and a discrete hallucinatory scene.

"Sounds of people talking ceased to be words and turned into pure sounds flowing around and through me" — concrete auditory perceptual alteration. Matches ambient-alteration criterion established by AB scenes.
Ambient perceptual amplification without a discrete hallucinatory object — the Guidelines do not rule cleanly on whether brightness/colour/shimmer changes alone constitute a scene.

"Visions plentiful but solemn; mostly in black and white" + "ego being physically crushed like a can". Mixed passage: summary-of-visions (no specific scene) + ego-metaphor. Guidelines unclear on whether summary statements and ego-metaphors constitute discrete scenes.

"Friend who told frog joke shows up in frog form a few times during the session" — granularity edge-case relative to AB S10 ("two friends appear in fractal space: one as a frog, one dancing"). May be covered by S10 in B's individuation, or a separate earlier instance.

"Living, breathing Earth with water flowing around and through it" — concrete cosmic visual hallucination. Directly matches AB S08 ("beautiful fractal rainbow spiral world") — same cosmic-vision category.
Fragment/sub-scene of a holistic scene coded by the other rater — the Guidelines do not specify lump-vs-split granularity for overlapping spans.
Fragment/sub-scene of a holistic scene coded by the other rater — the Guidelines do not specify lump-vs-split granularity for overlapping spans.

"Own consciousness manifests as loosely defined part of the fractal; candle-light fading" — subjective phenomenology of consciousness within the already-individuated fractal world. Not an additional perceptual scene; more a felt-quality.

"Normal human waking life as one small dark loop in the pattern" — concrete imagery attached to an insight about human life. Edge-case between hallucinated imagery and metaphorical vision; Guidelines unclear on where to draw the line.

"Waves rose slowly and rainbow pastel colors started to separate and replicate" — concrete ambient visual hallucination. Matches the pair's criterion on lights dancing / 3D mosaic; early-onset scene B may have undervalued.
"Gave birth to the universe, accompanied by ecstatic dancing" — ecstatic/spiritual experience with metaphorical content. Not a perceptual hallucinatory scene.
"Cosmic winds blew through the center of where I had been until there was nothing" — ego-dissolution metaphor, no perceptual content. Classic trip-level phenomenon.

"When I turned my gaze, darkness followed behind, but what I looked at was illuminated" — concrete visual-field alteration (anomalous light/dark distribution relative to gaze). Matches AB S03 ("lights dancing; poster is a 3D mosaic undulating"): ambient-visual alteration category.

"Still-distorting houses and telephone poles seen from ambulance" — concrete visual distortion continuing at a new location. Direct continuation of AB S02 ("outlines wavy/melting") and S05 ("everything in visual field was breathing").
"With the pulsing came recognizable images from our own world" — summary-of-transition statement referencing S04's pulsing. Granularity edge-case: transition description vs discrete scene.

"Entity took my arms, poked me in the face/chest while repeating 'you are the universe'" — novel physical-interaction event with the entity established in AB S01/S03/S08. Distinct tactile+auditory+message content not covered by other AB scenes.
"Within 10 minutes things were getting real colorful" — early-onset brief general colour-shift summary. Guidelines unclear on whether brief onset-effect summaries constitute a scene.
Fragment/sub-scene of a holistic scene coded by the other rater — the Guidelines do not specify lump-vs-split granularity for overlapping spans.
Ambient perceptual amplification without a discrete hallucinatory object — the Guidelines do not rule cleanly on whether brightness/colour/shimmer changes alone constitute a scene.

"Watercolor of red apples on emerald green background boiled and moved in hypnotic chaotic madness" — concrete visual hallucination of a painting transforming. Matches AB S02/S07 (full kaleidoscope, swimming through geometric objects) — pair's criterion strongly covers surface-motion.
"House was very psychedelic and trippy" — general summary with no specific hallucinatory content. Guidelines do not specify whether generalised atmospheric statements constitute a scene.
"Room bursts into a million colors and crazy patterns" — ambient colour+pattern explosion. Matches AB criterion (many animated surfaces/patterns in this trip).

"Peeled banana, once peeled, had streaks of red and purple in it" — concrete visual alteration of specific object. Matches AB criterion (paintings come alive, blankets as clouds) — specific-object-hallucination category.

"Feel electricity in the back of my head and my neck hair was standing up" during a storm — somatic sensation, possibly real (storm-related static / arousal) rather than hallucinatory. Edge-case for the hallucinatory-scene category.

"First flashes of pastel colors began to dance behind my eyelids" — minor onset closed-eye visual. Threshold edge-case: are minor onset CEVs discrete scenes or too-minor-to-individuate? Guidelines unclear.

Somatic self-transformation / interoceptive content — the passage is phenomenologically real but may not be a hallucinatory SCENE in the external-object sense; would fit better in a body-schema / ego-dissolution category the current taxonomy lacks.
"It was my turn to die" — insight/belief with no perceptual content. Would fit a delusional-belief or existential-insight category, not a hallucinatory scene.

"Limbs in the tree seemed to pull away from me and each other" — concrete visual distortion of trees. Directly matches AB S12/S13 (trees melting and bending); an earlier instance of the same phenomenon B consolidated later.

"The sun had this awesome presence, it was warm and powerful, a God-like figure" — subjective feeling about the sun's meaning/significance. No hallucinated visual alteration of the sun; numinous feeling rather than perception.

"Scenery had definitely changed, nothing seemed to sit in space as it normally would, and nothing was stationary" — summary statement of ongoing visual distortion. Granularity edge-case relative to AB S12/S13/S14 which describe the same state.

"Friends' yells reverberated the same way the hawk's scream did, and I felt them all around me" — concrete auditory distortion with spatial-dispersal content. Directly matches AB S11 ("yelps of coyotes and reverberating auditory hallucinations") — same phenomenon.

"Entered a strange world in my head; filled with language, numbers, confusion, loss ness" — inner cognitive/mental experience with no external perceptual content. Would fit a mentation-state category, not hallucinatory scene.
"I was not Gregg Dix; I was a primitive mind…" — delusional/existential-belief passage with no perceptual content. Classic ego-dissolution trip-level phenomenon.

"Poked my head from the blanket and it was a strange orange dawn-like place, it was night no longer" — concrete visual perception of altered environment. Matches AB S14 ("strange reality: street lights bent, cars flew through houses, stars fell") — same environment-alteration category.
"Trees outside loomed at him vibrant and alive" — visual alteration / anthropomorphisation of trees. Matches AB S01/S02 (bricks as carpet, numbers/face on wall) — ambient object-alteration category.

"Couldn't tell whether pictures on Alex Grey's website were actually moving animations or not" — metacognitive uncertainty about whether a perception is hallucinated or real. Edge-case: the scene-existence judgement itself is in the rater's uncertainty. Guidelines unclear on how to code "uncertain-origin" perceptions.

"Resistance between my fingers and the desk… had no location… just the sensation of pressure" — altered somatosensory processing (dimensionless tactile perception). Phenomenologically real but not a hallucinated content scene; would fit an altered-perceptual-phenomenology category.

"Language went too, devolving into glossolalia soup" + random-image conjuring — language dysfunction + internally-generated imagery. Language dysfunction is cognitive; the imagery is voluntary/conjured, not spontaneous hallucination.

"Newspapers on table defy gravity, flap gently as if from a breeze" — concrete visual hallucination of objects moving. Matches AB S06 ("Quetzalcoatl reappears, morphs room: walls split, windows become flapping mirrors") — same flapping/environment-distortion phenomenon.

"Outlines of spiders on both peaks of Squaw Peak" — concrete visual hallucination with specific content. Matches AB S03 ("fuzzy edge of scarf appears as large white spider") and S05 ("jaguar's eyes staring back from city skyline") — same pattern-outline hallucination pattern.
"Words were moving across the screen" — concrete visual alteration of text. Pair's criterion includes many surface-motion alterations throughout the trip.

"Circle of light on the roof is turning clockwise, then counter-clockwise" — concrete visual hallucination of motion. Pair's criterion elsewhere in neighbouring trips (Alessandra × Alessio) includes movement perception hallucinations.
"Portraits on wall seem pleased with my efforts to entertain the stern one" — animated portraits with responsive expressions. Concrete visual person-in-art hallucination.

"Outside feels like afterlife: still, void of life, worn down, no leaves" — atmospheric feeling attributing afterlife-meaning to real environmental features. Not a concrete hallucination; more a meaning-attribution / mood.

"Every word I say is influencing my reality" — magical-thinking / delusional belief about word-world coupling. Not a perceptual hallucination; a cognitive/belief state.

"Water from faucet splashes then seemed to freeze within seconds; time stops until track restarts" — concrete visual+temporal hallucination with specific content. Matches the only AB scene (body split) in having concrete anomalous perceptual content, and matches pair's criterion in neighbouring trips.

"Finger lights stay in my vision for many seconds after I would stop moving them" — concrete visual trail/after-image hallucination. Matches AB criterion (S11 houses morph, S12 guitar bending) — visual-distortion category.

"World seemed to fly by us; my legs didn't exist for a few moments as I floated" — concrete somatic+visual hallucination (legs vanish, floating). Matches AB S04 (ground bending under weight) and S13 (body morphing) — same body/environment alteration category.
Fragment/sub-scene of a holistic scene coded by the other rater — the Guidelines do not specify lump-vs-split granularity for overlapping spans.

"Electric jolt in mouth whenever the taste felt strongest" — somatic/gustatory sensation co-occurring with real taste. Edge-case: could be real nerve sensation from the mushroom or a somatic hallucination. Guidelines unclear on how to treat co-occurring-with-real-stimulus sensations.

"Could have sworn I pissed and shat all over my pants; later found perfectly clean — must have hallucinated that part" — a false somatic/proprioceptive belief corrected later. Edge-case between somatic hallucination and a delusional false belief.

"Lights fade in/out slowly with 1-minute delay after switch is flipped" — concrete temporal/visual distortion. Matches AB criterion (S11 houses morph, S12 guitar bending over time) — temporal-visual distortion category.
